commentaries.author: Irenaeus of Lyons
commentaries.content: For, speaking no longer by the Spirit of God, but setting up another law of fornication contrary to the law of God,

commentaries.author: Tertullian of Carthage
commentaries.content:
John, however, in the Apocalypse is charged to chastise those "who eat things sacrificed to idols "and "who commit fornication.".
Censures the Pergamenes for teaching perverse things;

commentaries.author: Victorinus of Pettau
commentaries.content: That is, I will say what I shall command, and I will tell you what you shall do. For Balaam, with his doctrine, taught Balak to cast a stumbling-block before the eyes of the children of Israel, to eat what was sacrificed to idols, and to commit fornication—a thing which is known to have happened of old. For he gave this advice to the king of the Moabites, and they caused stumbling to the people. Thus, says He, you have among you those who hold such doctrine; and under the pretext of mercy, you would corrupt others.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
